HypothesisName: High Usage Big vs Stingy DREB
Theory: Public lines can overprice high-usage rebounders when matchup environment suppresses rebound chances.
PromotionRule: Promote if sample >= 30, WR >= 56%, ROI > 5%, and CLV >= 0.
RetirementRule: Downgrade if sample >= 20 and WR < 48% or ROI < -5%.
ResearchNotes: Good results but negative CLV. Possible variance or market disagreement. Keep tracking.

Theory: Athletic wings/forwards can exceed rebound lines against poor defensive rebounding teams.
PromotionRule: Promote if sample >= 20, WR >= 55%, ROI > 5%, and CLV > 0.
ResearchNotes: Excellent early result but tiny sample. Strong research candidate.

Theory: Overs without strong matchup/minutes/role support are likely overfit or narrative-driven.
PromotionRule: Only revive with clearly defined role/matchup conditions.
RetirementRule: Retire if sample >= 5 and ROI remains negative.
ResearchNotes: Bad early result. Keep downgraded until better definition exists.

Theory: Strong defensive rebounding environments may suppress second-chance points and support unders.
PromotionRule: Needs stronger supporting diagnostics: pace negative, eFG negative, and injury/rotation confirmation.

Theory: Playoff physicality plus elite defensive rebounding may create under value in Portuguese basketball.
PromotionRule: No promotion until LPB market lines and team stats are structured.
ResearchNotes: Interesting but not expandable until LPB data coverage improves.
